mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes that provides a comprehensive set of tools for building, deploying, and managing machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for AI practitioners and platform administrators looking to leverage Kubernetes for scalable AI solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow command-line interface (CLI) to help manage your Kubeflow installation.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step installs Kubeflow using the CLI, which deploys the necessary components.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow to the cluster.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your Kubeflow installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/rbac/rbac.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring.
> Set up RBAC.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Remove Kubeflow components.
> Delete the GKE cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the installation instructions from the Kubernetes documentation.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'kubeflow-system' namespace are not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check resource limits and ensure the cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow installation was not successful or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation logs and ensure you are using the correct namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30, and enhanced user interface.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to migrate your existing pipelines and configurations as per the migration guide.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
